description: Replace request-body-based relational write response ETag generation in `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend/DefaultRelationalWriteExecutor.cs` with an in-session materialization of the committed external representation. Acceptance criteria: relational-table POST create, POST-as-update, PUT, and guarded no-op success paths no longer call `RelationalApiMetadataFormatter.FormatEtag(...)` on `SelectedBody`; the returned ETag is derived from the same external-response JSON shape used by relational GET-by-id; the ETag remains stable with the committed write even if `SelectedBody` is only the writable subset of the eventual response document.
steps_to_verify: Add unit coverage proving the executor returns the materialized response ETag rather than `FormatEtag(request.SelectedBody, ...)`.
Add a regression case where the committed external document includes fields not present in `SelectedBody`, and assert the returned ETag matches the committed external document.

description: Introduce a write-side committed-representation readback flow that reuses the relational hydration and read materialization pipeline inside the active write session. Acceptance criteria: the executor can materialize the committed external response for a just-written relational-table document before commit completes; the readback materializes the full external GET shape used for relational GET-by-id, including descriptor URI projection; the implementation does not perform a post-commit repository GET to compute the response ETag; the readback runs in the same transaction/session boundary as the write so the returned ETag cannot be changed by a concurrent writer after commit.
steps_to_verify: Return or otherwise retain the committed target document identity needed to hydrate the written document inside the executor session.
Add unit tests proving the committed-response readback is invoked from the write executor success path and is not replaced by a second post-commit lookup.

description: Plumb the resolved root `DocumentId` or equivalent committed target identity out of `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend/RelationalWriteNoProfilePersister.cs` so the executor can hydrate the just-written document without a second UUID-to-target lookup. Acceptance criteria: create-new and existing-document write paths both expose enough committed target identity to drive in-session hydration; the executor does not need to re-resolve the target after the write succeeds just to compute the response ETag.
steps_to_verify: Add unit tests covering create-new, POST-as-update, and PUT paths to prove the executor receives the final committed target identity needed for readback.

description: Remove the unused `ContentVersion` readback from `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend/DefaultRelationalWriteExecutor.cs` once the committed-response readback is in place. Acceptance criteria: `ReadCommittedContentVersionAsync(...)` and its call sites are deleted unless they become part of the real committed-response materialization path; there is no extra database roundtrip whose result is discarded; write success behavior is unchanged except for the corrected ETag source.
steps_to_verify: Delete the dead helper and update unit tests to assert no discarded `ContentVersion` readback remains in the success path.

description: Remove unused `ContentVersion` return plumbing from `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend/DescriptorWriteHandler.cs`. Acceptance criteria: descriptor POST and PUT SQL no longer reshape statements solely to return `ContentVersion`; `ExecuteContentVersionCommandAsync(...)` is removed unless repurposed for an actively consumed result; descriptor create, update, and no-op behavior stays the same from the caller perspective, including current descriptor response ETag behavior.
steps_to_verify: Update descriptor handler unit tests so they no longer depend on a returned `ContentVersion` result set for successful writes.

category: Repository And Handler Contract Regression Coverage
description: Update repository, handler, and HTTP-surface tests to assert the corrected committed-response ETag contract instead of the current request-body-derived behavior. Acceptance criteria: unit tests no longer encode `SelectedBody` as the source of truth for write response ETags; Core handlers continue emitting the backend-provided ETag in the HTTP response; descriptor write tests do not assert or mock discarded `ContentVersion` results.
steps_to_verify: Update unit tests in backend and core projects that currently compute expected ETags from the selected request body.

category: Write Then Read ETag Parity Integration Tests
description: Add integration coverage proving a successful relational write returns the same ETag that a follow-up relational GET-by-id returns for the committed document. Acceptance criteria: at least one relational-table create/update/no-op scenario verifies write-result ETag equals GET-by-id `_etag` for the same committed state; the fixture exercises a shape where the external response is meaningfully materialized, not just a trivial root-only document; descriptor write integration coverage continues to prove descriptor update tracking behavior without depending on unused `ContentVersion` readback.
steps_to_verify: Add integration assertions for relational-table write-result ETag parity with subsequent GET-by-id `_etag` on PostgreSQL and, where the fixture already exists, SQL Server.
